hero.headline: Dr. MGR Online MBA in Human Resource Management: Fees, Syllabus and Career Scope
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved 2-year programme | NAAC A+ accredited | Rs 1,36,000 - Rs 1,41,000 total fee | Deemed University since 2003

sections.tldr: Dr. MGR Educational and Research Institute Online MBA in Human Resource Management covers Training and Development and Stress Management in Sem 3. Common Sem 2 includes Talent Management, Strategic HRM, and Industrial Relations as HR-adjacent subjects. Common Sem 4 core includes Digital Marketing, International Business Management, and an 8-week mandatory Field Work and Project Work. Total fee Rs 1,36,000 to Rs 1,41,000 across 4 semesters. NAAC A+ accredited, UGC-DEB approved, Deemed University.

about.body:
Dr. MGR Educational and Research Institute is a Deemed University in Chennai, Tamil Nadu, named after former Chief Minister Dr. M.G. Ramachandran. The institution has operated under UGC recognition since 2003 and holds NAAC A+ accreditation with UGC-DEB approval for online programmes.

The Human Resource Management specialisation adds Training and Development and Stress Management as Sem 3 spec-specific courses. The HR depth extends into Sem 2 common core, which includes Talent Management, Strategic HRM, and Industrial Relations as HR-adjacent subjects. This means the HR exposure spans Semesters 2 through 3.

Sem 4 shares common core subjects across all MBA specialisations, including the 8-week mandatory Field Work and Project Work. For HR candidates, this field project can be completed in an HR department, covering recruitment processes, training programme design, or employee engagement initiatives.

At Rs 1,36,000 to Rs 1,41,000 total, the programme sits in the mid-range for NAAC A+ online MBAs. Dr. MGR's Deemed University status since 2003 and Chennai location provide strong recognition in Tamil Nadu and South India.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1-2 cover the common MBA core. Common Sem 2 includes Talent Management, Strategic HRM, and Industrial Relations as HR-adjacent subjects. Semesters 3-4 below show the HRM specialisation subjects. Spec-specific subjects listed first, followed by common core.

subjects.name: Training and Development
subjects.desc: Training needs analysis, programme design, delivery methods, e-learning integration, ROI measurement, and competency-based training frameworks.

subjects.name: Stress Management
subjects.desc: Workplace stress identification, occupational health, employee wellness programmes, burnout prevention, and organizational stress intervention strategies.

subjects.name: Strategic Management
subjects.desc: Competitive strategy frameworks, industry analysis, corporate-level strategy, and strategic implementation processes.

subjects.name: Entrepreneurship Development
subjects.desc: Startup ideation, business plan creation, funding strategies, MSME support systems, and entrepreneurial risk management.

subjects.name: Management Accounting
subjects.desc: Cost-volume-profit analysis, budgetary control, standard costing, variance analysis, and management decision-making tools.

subjects.name: Production and Operations Management
subjects.desc: Production planning, quality management, facility layout, capacity planning, and operations scheduling.

subjects.name: Skill Development
subjects.desc: Communication skills, presentation techniques, team management, and professional development modules.

subjects.name: Summer Project
subjects.desc: Industry-linked summer project in an HR function requiring field research, data collection, and structured report.

subjects.name: Digital Marketing
subjects.desc: Digital marketing fundamentals, employer branding online, recruitment marketing, and social media strategy.

subjects.name: International Business Management
subjects.desc: Global HR practices, cross-cultural management, international labour laws, and expatriate management.

subjects.name: Contemporary Seminar
subjects.desc: Student-led seminar on current HR management topics. Research, presentation, and peer discussion.

subjects.name: Field Work and Project Work (8 Weeks)
subjects.desc: Mandatory 8-week field project in an HR department or consulting firm. Structured report, data analysis, and viva voce defence.

whoHires.body:
Dr. MGR MBA Human Resource Management graduates target roles across HR operations, talent management, and organizational development:

**Industries:** IT/ITES, manufacturing, banking, healthcare (leveraging Dr. MGR's heritage), retail, consulting, staffing agencies, and government departments

**Entry-level roles:** HR Executive, Recruitment Coordinator, Training Coordinator, Payroll Executive, Employee Engagement Associate, HR Operations Associate

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years):** HR Manager, Talent Acquisition Manager, Training and Development Manager, Compensation and Benefits Manager, HRBP (HR Business Partner), Employee Relations Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** Head of HR, CHRO (mid-market), VP Human Resources, Director of Talent Management, Head of Learning and Development

**Employers:** IT companies (TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HCL in Chennai), manufacturing firms, hospitals and healthcare chains (Apollo, MIOT, Kauvery), banking (SBI, HDFC, ICICI), and Tamil Nadu government departments.

**Note:** Chennai is India's fourth-largest IT hub. Dr. MGR's local brand recognition gives graduates access to the Chennai/Tamil Nadu HR job market.

skills.body:
The Human Resource Management specialisation builds skills across training, employee wellness, and talent management:

**Technical skills:**
- Training and Development: needs analysis frameworks (Kirkpatrick model), programme design, e-learning platform evaluation, training ROI calculation, and competency mapping
- Stress Management: workplace stress assessment tools, employee assistance programme (EAP) design, burnout indicators, wellness programme metrics, and occupational health compliance
- Talent Management (from Sem 2): talent pipeline building, succession planning, performance management systems, and high-potential identification

**Applied skills:**
- Strategic HRM (from Sem 2): HR strategy alignment with business goals, workforce planning, HR analytics basics, and organizational design
- Industrial Relations (from Sem 2): Trade union management, collective bargaining, Industrial Disputes Act, and labour compliance

**Soft skills:** Conducting training sessions, employee counselling, conflict resolution, exit interview management, and presenting HR metrics to leadership.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Dr. MGR Online MBA Human Resource Management against peer programmes:

**vs SMU Online MBA HRM (Rs 1,20,000):** SMU is Rs 16,000 to Rs 21,000 cheaper with the Manipal group brand. SMU has established pan-India placement infrastructure. Dr. MGR has NAAC A+ (same tier) and the 8-week field project. SMU wins on brand and fee. Dr. MGR wins on project depth and TN regional recognition.

**vs Galgotias Online MBA HR (Rs 86,400):** Galgotias is Rs 49,600 to Rs 54,600 cheaper. Both are NAAC A+. Galgotias has NCR location advantage. Dr. MGR has Deemed University heritage and the 8-week field project. Galgotias wins clearly on fee. Dr. MGR wins for South India candidates.

**vs Uttaranchal Online MBA HR (Rs 94,000):** Uttaranchal is Rs 42,000 to Rs 47,000 cheaper. Both are UGC-DEB approved. Uttaranchal has North India (Dehradun) location. Dr. MGR has NAAC A+ and Chennai/TN brand. Choose based on regional career focus.

fees.body:
The Dr. MGR Online MBA in Human Resource Management is priced between Rs 1,36,000 and Rs 1,41,000 total across 4 semesters (2-year programme). Fee range varies slightly by specialization - contact our counsellor for the exact figure for your chosen track.

**Payment breakdown**:
- **Per semester**: Rs 34,000 - Rs 40,000 across 4 semesters
- **Full upfront option**: Rs 1,36,000 - Rs 1,41,000 one-time

**Additional charges**:
- Registration fee: Rs 1,000 (one-time, non-refundable at admission)

**Zero-cost EMI**: Available directly through Dr. MGR Educational and Research Institute starting Rs 5,700 per month. No external marketing partner or NBFC intermediary.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Dr. MGR Online each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees for Human Resource Management with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Human Resource Management as the specialisation, issued by Dr. MGR Educational and Research Institute (Deemed University) under UGC-DEB online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A+ accreditation, UGC-DEB approval, and Deemed University status since 2003. Valid for HR manager roles, government recruitment, PSU HR positions, and private-sector human resources careers. DigiLocker issuance is standard.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from Dr. MGR Online MBA Human Resource Management students. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Meena
items.liked: Training and Development subject was directly useful for my L&D role at a Chennai IT company. Dr. MGR's local brand recognition helped during internal promotion discussions
items.disliked: Only two HRM-specific subjects in Sem 3. Sem 4 has no HR-specific courses at all
items.body: I chose Dr. MGR because the name carries weight in Chennai corporate circles. NAAC A+ at this fee is reasonable. Training and Development covered Kirkpatrick model and ROI measurement at a practical level. Stress Management was relevant for my employee wellness initiatives. The 8-week HR field project added structure. The gap is limited spec-specific depth: only two dedicated HR subjects in Sem 3.

items.name: Arun
items.liked: Stress Management as a standalone subject is uncommon in online MBA HRM programmes. Directly applicable to my role managing factory floor employee wellness
items.disliked: Pan-India brand recognition is weak compared to Amity, Manipal, or LPU. Northern employers rarely know Dr. MGR
items.body: The HRM programme is compact. Two spec subjects in Sem 3, plus Sem 2 covers Talent Management, Strategic HRM, and Industrial Relations as common core. So the HR exposure is spread across Sem 2-3. The 8-week field project was the highlight. I completed it in my manufacturing unit's HR department. For Tamil Nadu and South India HR careers, Dr. MGR is a solid choice. For national ambitions, the brand limitation is real.

items.name: Kavitha
items.liked: The Sem 2 common core subjects (Talent Management, Strategic HRM, Industrial Relations) provided more HR depth than expected from a general MBA structure
items.disliked: Fee is higher than Galgotias (Rs 86,400) and Uttaranchal (Rs 94,000) for similar NAAC-tier HR programmes
items.body: At Rs 1.36-1.41L, Dr. MGR is not the cheapest option for an HR MBA. Galgotias and Uttaranchal are Rs 40,000-50,000 cheaper. Dr. MGR's value is the Tamil Nadu brand and Deemed University status since 2003. If you are a working professional in TN who needs the MBA credential for promotion, Dr. MGR delivers. If fee is your top criterion, look at cheaper alternatives.

faqs.question: Is Dr. MGR a Deemed University?
faqs.answer: Yes. Dr. MGR Educational and Research Institute has operated as a Deemed University under UGC recognition since 2003. Named after former Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Dr. M.G. Ramachandran.

faqs.question: What is the fee difference between Rs 1.36L and Rs 1.41L?
faqs.answer: The total fee varies slightly by specialization. Contact our counsellor to confirm the exact fee for Human Resource Management before making any payment.

faqs.question: Is Dr. MGR Online MBA UGC-DEB approved?
faqs.answer: Yes. Dr. MGR Educational and Research Institute is UGC-DEB approved for online programmes. The degree is legally equivalent to a campus MBA under UGC 2020 guidelines.

faqs.question: Does the Sem 2 common core include HR subjects?
faqs.answer: Yes. Common Sem 2 includes Talent Management, Strategic HRM, and Industrial Relations. These are shared across all MBA specialisations but provide foundational HR depth before the Sem 3 specialisation subjects.

faqs.question: Can the 8-week field project be done in an HR department?
faqs.answer: Yes. HRM students typically complete the 8-week field project in an HR department, staffing firm, or consulting organization. The project can cover recruitment, training, employee engagement, or any HR function.

chooseIf: You are based in Tamil Nadu or South India where Dr. MGR's brand carries strong corporate recognition.
Training and Development and Stress Management as dedicated subjects align with your L&D or employee wellness career.
NAAC A+ at a mid-range fee (Rs 1.36-1.41L) meets your requirements.
The 8-week mandatory field project in an HR department is valuable for your career development.
consider: Galgotias at Rs 86,400 or Uttaranchal at Rs 94,000 offer HR MBAs at significantly lower fees with similar NAAC grades.
SMU at Rs 1,20,000 has stronger pan-India brand recognition with the Manipal group backing.
Only two HRM-specific subjects in Sem 3. If you want deeper HR specialisation depth, compare syllabi with SMU or Amity.
National brand recognition outside Tamil Nadu is limited. For North India HR careers, consider Manipal, Amity, or CU.
